normalized_title: State Planning Policy 3.5 Historic Heritage Conservation
scope_summary: State Planning Policy made under Part 3 of the Planning and Development Act 2005 setting out principles for the conservation and protection of Western Australia's historic cultural heritage (heritage areas, buildings and structures, historic cemeteries and gardens, man-made landscapes, historic/archaeological sites) at State and local level. It applies throughout Western Australia and guides identification and assessment of heritage places, designation of heritage areas, establishment of heritage lists, relevant considerations and development control principles for development assessment affecting heritage places (alterations, extensions, change of use, demolition, infill within heritage areas), and integration of heritage into planning schemes and strategies. Aboriginal heritage and natural heritage are excluded except where they intersect with registered or listed historic heritage.

cross_references.cited: Model Scheme Text (Town Planning Amendment Regulations 1999)
